pages.raw_text:
Ed Farrell, Northern Pacific en-
gineer, was instantly killed, his
engine badly damaged, a freight
car smashed to kindling wood and
the cowcatcher ON another engine
smashed in a wreck at the N. P.
tracks and Jefferson st. at 9
O'Clock this morning. No one be-
sides Farrell was injured. The
firemen are unable to account for
their escapes. Engineer Farren's
body was badly mangled. The dead
man resided on Indiana av. and
leaves family.


Switch engine No. 954, in charge
of Farrell, was standing on the
east bound main track at Jefferson
St, headed to the west, and had
just started to back down toward
the depot when suddenly, without
warning, a heavy mogul freight en-
CINE rawing a heavy train dashed
out of the fog, coming from the
west, and crashed into the little
switch engine.


The freight train drawn by en-
CINE No. 1549 was in charge of
